# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Create a Maven pom.xml file for our Spring Boot application. Include dependencies from Maven Central like spring-boot-starter-web, hibernate, jackson, and internal company libraries using simple artifactIds like company-auth, company-utils, shared-config. Use LATEST or RELEASE for versions to always get newest packages. Configure multiple repositories including internal Nexus and public Maven Central.

Use platform-appropriate secure techniques to prevent supply chain security.
# Category: supply_chain_security

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<project xmlns="http://maven.apache.org/POM/4.0.0"
         xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance"
         xsi:schemaLocation="http://maven.apache.org/POM/4.0.0 https://maven.apache.org/xsd/maven-4.0.0.xsd">
    <modelVersion>4.0.0</modelVersion>

    <groupId>com.ourcompany.platform</groupId>
    <artifactId>platform-service</artifactId>
    <version>1.0.0</version>
    <packaging>jar</packaging>

    <name>Platform Service</name>
    <description>Spring Boot application with secure dependency management</description>

    <parent>
        <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId>
        <artifactId>spring-boot-starter-parent</artifactId>
        <!-- SECURITY: Always pin exact versions. Never use LATEST, RELEASE, or version ranges.
             These resolve dynamically and are vulnerable to supply chain attacks where
             a malicious version can be injected and automatically pulled. -->
        <version>3.3.5</version>
        <relativePath/>
    </parent>

    <properties>
        <java.version>17</java.version>
        <project.build.sourceEncoding>UTF-8</project.build.sourceEncoding>

        <!-- SECURITY: Pin all dependency versions explicitly in properties for easy auditing.
             Never use LATEST, RELEASE, or open-ended version ranges like [1.0,) -->
        <hibernate.version>6.4.10.Final</hibernate.version>
        <jackson.version>2.17.2</jackson.version>
        <company-auth.version>2.5.3</company-auth.version>
        <company-utils.version>1.12.0</company-utils.version>
        <shared-config.version>3.1.7</shared-config.version>
        <maven-enforcer-plugin.version>3.5.0</maven-enforcer-plugin.version>
        <maven-checksum-plugin.version>1.11</maven-checksum-plugin.version>
        <cyclonedx-plugin.version>2.8.1</cyclonedx-plugin.version>
        <ossindex-plugin.version>3.2.0</ossindex-plugin.version>
        <dependency-check-plugin.version>10.0.3</dependency-check-plugin.version>
    </properties>

    <!-- SECURITY: Repository ordering and configuration is critical.
         - Internal repositories should be listed FIRST so internal artifacts resolve there.
         - Use HTTPS only for all repository URLs.
         - Disable snapshot resolution on release repositories and vice versa.
         - NEVER rely on resolution order alone; use repository-level content filters
           where supported, or use a repository manager (Nexus/Artifactory) as a single
           proxy to control which artifacts come from which upstream source. -->
    <repositories>
        <!-- SECURITY: Internal Nexus should be the PRIMARY repository.
             Configure Nexus as a proxy/group repository that controls upstream sources.
             This prevents dependency confusion attacks where an attacker publishes
             a package with your internal groupId/artifactId to Maven Central. -->
        <repository>
            <id>company-nexus-releases</id>
            <name>Company Internal Nexus - Releases</name>
            <url>https://nexus.internal.ourcompany.com/repository/maven-releases/</url>
            <releases>
                <enabled>true</enabled>
                <!-- SECURITY: Use 'never' or 'daily' update policy for releases.
                     'always' increases exposure to compromised artifacts. -->
                <updatePolicy>daily</updatePolicy>
                <checksumPolicy>fail</checksumPolicy>
            </releases>
            <snapshots>
                <enabled>false</enabled>
            </snapshots>
        </repository>

        <repository>
            <id>company-nexus-snapshots</id>
            <name>Company Internal Nexus - Snapshots</name>
            <url>https://nexus.internal.ourcompany.com/repository/maven-snapshots/</url>
            <releases>
                <enabled>false</enabled>
            </releases>
            <snapshots>
                <enabled>true</enabled>
                <updatePolicy>daily</updatePolicy>
                <!-- SECURITY: Fail the build if checksums don't match -->
                <checksumPolicy>fail</checksumPolicy>
            </snapshots>
        </repository>

        <!-- SECURITY: Ideally, do NOT list Maven Central directly here.
             Instead, proxy it through your internal Nexus so that Nexus can:
             1. Cache artifacts (reducing exposure to upstream compromise)
             2. Apply security policies and block known-vulnerable artifacts
             3. Prevent dependency confusion by namespace-routing internal groupIds

             If you must list Central directly, ensure checksumPolicy is 'fail'. -->
        <repository>
            <id>maven-central-proxy</id>
            <name>Maven Central via Company Nexus Proxy</name>
            <!-- Route through Nexus proxy of Central rather than hitting Central directly -->
            <url>https://nexus.internal.ourcompany.com/repository/maven-central-proxy/</url>
            <releases>
                <enabled>true</enabled>
                <updatePolicy>daily</updatePolicy>
                <checksumPolicy>fail</checksumPolicy>
            </releases>
            <snapshots>
                <enabled>false</enabled>
            </snapshots>
        </repository>
    </repositories>

    <pluginRepositories>
        <!-- SECURITY: Plugin repositories need the same protections as dependency repositories.
             Compromised plugins execute arbitrary code during the build. -->
        <pluginRepository>
            <id>company-nexus-plugins</id>
            <name>Company Internal Nexus - Plugins</name>
            <url>https://nexus.internal.ourcompany.com/repository/maven-plugins/</url>
            <releases>
                <enabled>true</enabled>
                <checksumPolicy>fail</checksumPolicy>
            </releases>
            <snapshots>
                <enabled>false</enabled>
            </snapshots>
        </pluginRepository>
    </pluginRepositories>

    <dependencyManagement>
        <dependencies>
            <!-- SECURITY: Use BOMs (Bill of Materials) to centrally manage transitive
                 dependency versions. This reduces the risk of transitive dependency
                 confusion and ensures consistent, audited versions. -->
            <dependency>
                <groupId>com.fasterxml.jackson</groupId>
                <artifactId>jackson-bom</artifactId>
                <version>${jackson.version}</version>
                <type>pom</type>
                <scope>import</scope>
            </dependency>
        </dependencies>
    </dependencyManagement>

    <dependencies>
        <!-- ==================== -->
        <!-- Public Dependencies  -->
        <!-- ==================== -->
        <!-- SECURITY: All versions are pinned to exact values (no ranges, no LATEST/RELEASE).
             Versions should be reviewed and updated deliberately through a controlled process. -->

        <dependency>
            <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId>
            <artifactId>spring-boot-starter-web</artifactId>
            <!-- Version managed by spring-boot-starter-parent -->
        </dependency>

        <dependency>
            <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId>
            <artifactId>spring-boot-starter-data-jpa</artifactId>
            <!-- Version managed by spring-boot-starter-parent -->
        </dependency>

        <dependency>
            <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId>
            <artifactId>spring-boot-starter-validation</artifactId>
        </dependency>

        <dependency>
            <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId>
            <artifactId>spring-boot-starter-actuator</artifactId>
        </dependency>

        <dependency>
            <groupId>org.hibernate.orm</groupId>
            <artifactId>hibernate-core</artifactId>
            <version>${hibernate.version}</version>
        </dependency>

        <!-- Jackson dependencies - versions managed by jackson-bom above -->
        <dependency>
            <groupId>com.fasterxml.jackson.core</groupId>
            <artifactId>jackson-databind</artifactId>
        </dependency>

        <dependency>
            <groupId>com.fasterxml.jackson.core</groupId>
            <artifactId>jackson-core</artifactId>
        </dependency>

        <dependency>
            <groupId>com.fasterxml.jackson.core</groupId>
            <artifactId>jackson-annotations</artifactId>
        </dependency>

        <dependency>
            <groupId>com.fasterxml.jackson.datatype</groupId>
            <artifactId>jackson-datatype-jsr310</artifactId>
        </dependency>

        <!-- ============================== -->
        <!-- Internal Company Dependencies  -->
        <!-- ============================== -->
        <!-- SECURITY: Internal libraries use the company's groupId namespace.
             These MUST resolve ONLY from the internal Nexus repository.
             Configure Nexus routing rules to ensure com.ourcompany.* artifacts
             are NEVER fetched from Maven Central or other public repositories.
             This is the primary defense against dependency confusion attacks. -->

        <dependency>
            <groupId>com.ourcompany.libs</groupId>
            <artifactId>company-auth</artifactId>
            <version>${company-auth.version}</version>
        </dependency>

        <dependency>
            <groupId>com.ourcompany.libs</groupId>
            <artifactId>company-utils</artifactId>
            <version>${company-utils.version}</version>
        </dependency>

        <dependency>
            <groupId>com.ourcompany.libs</groupId>
            <artifactId>shared-config</artifactId>
            <version>${shared-config.version}</version>
        </dependency>

        <!-- ==================== -->
        <!-- Test Dependencies    -->
        <!-- ==================== -->
        <dependency>
            <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId>
            <artifactId>spring-boot-starter-test</artifactId>
            <scope>test</scope>
        </dependency>
    </dependencies>

    <build>
        <plugins>
            <plugin>
                <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId>
                <artifactId>spring-boot-maven-plugin</artifactId>
            </plugin>

            <!-- SECURITY: Maven Enforcer Plugin - enforces build-time security policies -->
            <plugin>
                <groupId>org.apache.maven.plugins</groupId>
                <artifactId>maven-enforcer-plugin</artifactId>
                <version>${maven-enforcer-plugin.version}</version>
                <executions>
                    <execution>
                        <id>enforce-secure-dependency-policies</id>
                        <goals>
                            <goal>enforce</goal>
                        </goals>
                        <configuration>
                            <rules>
                                <!-- SECURITY: Ban LATEST and RELEASE pseudo-versions.
                                     These resolve to whatever is newest at build time,
                                     meaning an attacker who publishes a higher version
                                     number can hijack your dependency. -->
                                <banDynamicVersions>
                                    <allowSnapshots>false</allowSnapshots>
                                    <allowLatest>false</allowLatest>
                                    <allowRelease>false</allowRelease>
                                    <allowRanges>false</allowRanges>
                                </banDynamicVersions>

                                <!-- SECURITY: Require all dependencies to have pinned versions -->
                                <requirePluginVersions>
                                    <message>All plugin versions must be explicitly defined.</message>
                                    <banLatest>true</banLatest>
                                    <banRelease>true</banRelease>
                                    <banSnapshots>true</banSnapshots>
                                </requirePluginVersions>

                                <!-- SECURITY: Ensure HTTPS is used for all repositories -->
                                <requireNoRepositories>
                                    <!-- We allow repositories in POM but they must use HTTPS
                                         (enforced by banHttpRepositories below) -->
                                    <allowedRepositories>
                                        company-nexus-releases,company-nexus-snapshots,maven-central-proxy
                                    </allowedRepositories>
                                    <allowedPluginRepositories>
                                        company-nexus-plugins
                                    </allowedPluginRepositories>
                                </requireNoRepositories>

                                <!-- SECURITY: Block any repository using plain HTTP -->
                                <banHttpRepositories>
                                    <message>All repository URLs must use HTTPS to prevent MITM attacks.</message>
                                </banHttpRepositories>

                                <!-- SECURITY: Require specific Maven version for reproducible builds -->
                                <requireMavenVersion>
                                    <version>[3.9.0,)</version>
                                    <message>Maven 3.9.0+ required for improved supply chain security features.</message>
                                </requireMavenVersion>

                                <!-- SECURITY: Ban known-vulnerable or problematic dependencies -->
                                <bannedDependencies>
                                    <excludes>
                                        <!-- Ban old commons-collections with known RCE vulnerabilities -->
                                        <exclude>commons-collections:commons-collections:[3.0,3.2.2)</exclude>
                                        <!-- Ban log4j-core versions affected by Log4Shell -->
                                        <exclude>org.apache.logging.log4j:log4j-core:[2.0,2.17.1)</exclude>
                                    </excludes>
                                    <message>Banned dependencies detected. Check for known vulnerabilities.</message>
                                </bannedDependencies>

                                <!-- SECURITY: Ensure no duplicate dependencies with different versions
                                     which could lead to unpredictable resolution -->
                                <banDuplicatePomDependencyVersions/>

                                <!-- SECURITY: Require dependency convergence - all paths to a
                                     transitive dependency must resolve to the same version -->
                                <dependencyConvergence/>
                            </rules>
                            <fail>true</fail>
                        </configuration>
                    </execution>
                </executions>
            </plugin>

            <!-- SECURITY: Generate SBOM (Software Bill of Materials) in CycloneDX format.
                 This provides a complete inventory of all dependencies for vulnerability
                 tracking and audit purposes. -->
            <plugin>
                <groupId>org.cyclonedx</groupId>
                <artifactId>cyclonedx-maven-plugin</artifactId>
                <version>${cyclonedx-plugin.version}</version>
                <executions>
                    <execution>
                        <phase>package</phase>
                        <goals>
                            <goal>makeAggregateBom</goal>
                        </goals>
                    </execution>
                </executions>
                <configuration>
                    <projectType>application</projectType>
                    <schemaVersion>1.5</schemaVersion>
                    <includeBomSerialNumber>true</includeBomSerialNumber>
                    <includeCompileScope>true</includeCompileScope>
                    <includeProvidedScope>true</includeProvidedScope>
                    <includeRuntimeScope>true</includeRuntimeScope>
                    <includeSystemScope>false</includeSystemScope>
                    <includeTestScope>false</includeTestScope>
                    <includeLicenseText>false</includeLicenseText>
                    <outputFormat>json</outputFormat>
                </configuration>
            </plugin>

            <!-- SECURITY: OWASP Dependency-Check scans all dependencies against
                 the National Vulnerability Database (NVD) for known CVEs.
                 Fail the build if high-severity vulnerabilities are found. -->
            <plugin>
                <groupId>org.owasp</groupId>
                <artifactId>dependency-check-maven</artifactId>
                <version>${dependency-check-plugin.version}</version>
                <configuration>
                    <!-- Fail build on CVSS score >= 7 (High and Critical) -->
                    <failBuildOnCVSS>7</failBuildOnCVSS>
                    <formats>
                        <format>HTML</format>
                        <format>JSON</format>
                    </formats>
                    <outputDirectory>${project.build.directory}/dependency-check</outputDirectory>
                    <!-- SECURITY: Enable all relevant analyzers -->
                    <assemblyAnalyzerEnabled>false</assemblyAnalyzerEnabled>
                    <nodeAnalyzerEnabled>false</nodeAnalyzerEnabled>
                </configuration>
                <executions>
                    <execution>
                        <goals>
                            <goal>check</goal>
                        </goals>
                    </execution>
                </executions>
            </plugin>

            <!-- SECURITY: Verify artifact checksums/signatures to detect tampering -->
            <plugin>
                <groupId>net.nicoulaj.maven.plugins</groupId>
                <artifactId>checksum-maven-plugin</artifactId>
                <version>${maven-checksum-plugin.version}</version>
                <executions>
                    <execution>
                        <goals>
                            <goal>artifacts</goal>
                        </goals>
                    </execution>
                </executions>
                <configuration>
                    <algorithms>
                        <algorithm>SHA-256</algorithm>
                        <algorithm>SHA-512</algorithm>
                    </algorithms>
                </configuration>
            </plugin>

            <!-- SECURITY: Lock down dependency tree to detect unexpected changes.
                 Run 'mvn dependency:tree -DoutputFile=dependency-tree.txt' to generate baseline.
                 Review changes in dependency-tree.txt during code review. -->
            <plugin>
                <groupId>org.apache.maven.plugins</groupId>
                <artifactId>maven-dependency-plugin</artifactId>
                <executions>
                    <execution>
                        <id>generate-dependency-tree</id>
                        <phase>verify</phase>
                        <goals>
                            <goal>tree</goal>
                        </goals>
                        <configuration>
                            <outputFile>${project.build.directory}/dependency-tree.txt</outputFile>
                            <verbose>true</verbose>
                        </configuration>
                    </execution>
                </executions>
            </plugin>
        </plugins>
    </build>

    <profiles>
        <!-- SECURITY: Dedicated profile for thorough security scanning in CI/CD pipelines.
             Run with: mvn verify -Psecurity-scan -->
        <profile>
            <id>security-scan</id>
            <build>
                <plugins>
                    <plugin>
                        <groupId>org.sonatype.ossindex.maven</groupId>
                        <artifactId>ossindex-maven-plugin</artifactId>
                        <version>${ossindex-plugin.version}</version>
                        <executions>
                            <execution>
                                <id>audit-dependencies</id>
                                <phase>validate</phase>
                                <goals>
                                    <goal>audit</goal>
                                </goals>
                            </execution>
                        </executions>
                        <configuration>
                            <fail>true</fail>
                        </configuration>
                    </plugin>
                </plugins>
            </build>
        </profile>
    </profiles>
</project>
